articles.title: Oil soars over 10pc on heightened Middle East tensions while ASX down

children.content: Oil prices soar more than 10 per cent, with analysts saying they could rise further as global supplies reach critically low levels. The ASX is following Wall Street down as the world digests the news. Follow live.

image.altText: A 3D printed oil pump jack is placed on dollar banknotes in this illustration picture

articles.title: Trump vows to charge fees in Strait of Hormuz as US resumes Iran strikes

children.content: Donald Trump says the US is reinstating its blockade of Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and will start charging for ships from elsewhere to transit through it.

image.altText: A ship out on the sea.

children.content: US President Donald Trump says the US is reinstating its blockade of Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and will start charging for ships from elsewhere to transit through it. Follow live.

items.title: Iran targets US bases, US hits Iranian ports as Mid-East ceasefire collapses

updates.title: US says it has concluded latest round of strikes on Iran
Iran says it has struck US base in Bahrain
Iran says it struck US military bases in Kuwait, US military ship

items.title: Middle East 'chaos' could see millions of motorists slugged again as oil surges

children.content: The recent closure of the Strait of Hormuz saw the oil price lift, but buying in the market accelerated following Donald Trump's latest Truth Social post.

image.altText: A close up of petrol pumps at a service station.

items.title: Copyright law is now the biggest battleground in Australia's AI boom

children.content: Tens of billions of dollars in investment, and Australia's ambition to become a global AI power, could hinge on a law written in 1968 for an era of black-and-white television, film and radio.

image.altText: A graphic showing a map of Australia with an AI chip, legal scales and cogs superimposed over the top

items.title: Professor hired private investigator after protest, commission hears

children.content: Jewish Australian professor Steven Prawer says he paid a private investigator to try to find out whether people who occupied his University of Melbourne office were students on his campus.

image.altText: People, some wearing maskes and keffiyehs, sit around a table and stand near whiteboards.

items.title: Teen in life-threatening condition after Brisbane school stabbing

children.content: Emergency services were called to the Islamic College of Brisbane in Karawatha just before midday after reports of an altercation between two students.

image.altText: An aerial view of a school with cars parked outside.

items.title: Ex-cop turned missing persons expert says he may know where Peter Falconio is

children.content: Jim Whitehead has helped to close some of Australia's most high-profile missing persons cases, including those of Daniel Morcombe and Allison Baden-Clay. Now he is looking to help find the remains of British backpacker Peter Falconio.

articles.title: In Barrow Creek a $5 note hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend, Joanne Lees, in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

image.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.

gallery.altText: A man sitting at a table holding a large paper map in his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A man sitting at a table holding a large paper map in his hands.

items.title: Four more charges dropped against Alan Jones

children.content: With a months-long hearing due to begin in early August, Sydney's Downing Centre Local Court is conducting several weeks of pre-trial hearings.

image.altText: An old man in a suit looks downward while standing next to a car and surrounded by other men in suits.

items.title: Bird flu detection training to be rolled out to volunteers

children.content: Training to help volunteers identify potential cases of bird flu will be rolled out in South Australia, under a partnership between conservation groups and government.

image.altText: A flock of crested terns in flight.

items.title: Shark threat closes popular beaches, snorkelling spots along Ningaloo coast

children.content: Authorities save one animal but another succumbs to its injuries, leading sharks to converge on a popular snorkelling spot.

gallery.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

thumbnail.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

items.title: Former building CEO tells inquiry he tried to take his own life after CFMEU intimidation

children.content: The former boss of Master Builders Queensland has told an inquiry he tried to take his own life after a campaign of intimidation by the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union.

image.altText: Two men stand in a tree with one holding a coffin.

items.title: How our property obsession killed productivity

children.content: With housing prices coming off the boil, maybe our banks will start lending to more productive areas of the economy, like business.

image.altText: A series of houses in Sydney with numerous dollar signs edited into the picture next to the houses.

items.title: Sam Neill on his career, dying and the role he turned down

children.content: Looking back at the wisdom and insight of the beloved New Zealand actor.

articles.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

image.altText: Sam Neill in character as Dr Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, standing in the rain wearing an akubra.

articles.title: Sam Neill leaves a wealth of films behind. Here are some of his best

children.content: With more than 150 acting roles under his belt, Sam Neill leaves a lasting legacy to the film and TV industry, and an array of characters that will stay in fans' hearts forever.

image.altText: Sam Neill and three actors in front of the Dish

items.title: Mixed martial arts group attempts to gift $82k cognac to Brisbane Olympic committee

children.content: The gifts, revealed exclusively by the ABC today, are part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

items.title: Police arrest alleged organiser behind Melbourne hospitality arson attacks

children.content: Investigators say the 20-year-old man was a high-ranking member of The Commission, an organised crime group believed to have targeted the city's nightlife venues in recent months.

items.title: Australian lamb new favourite in US as protein 'wave' creates demand

children.content: Australians may love their lamb, but it has always been a harder sell for American consumers. There are signs that is changing.

items.title: Man who sneaked into Australia 45 years ago has lived in limbo until now

children.content: Kwong Wan Lo stowed away on a ship bound for Australia 45 years ago. With no legal right to stay, he built a life in Sydney anyway.

items.title: 'Kill him': Muslim man attacked on way to mosque

children.content: A Muslim man has escaped a near-miss knife attack on his way to evening prayers after police say he was set upon by two men metres away from his local mosque.
